req.title: Delete patient (soft)
req.statement: A system-admin shall soft-delete a patient resource; deletion shall not remove history.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates a soft-delete on /Patient/{id} restricted to admin role, preserving history. Four test cases cover: (1) EP positive – admin successfully soft-deletes an active patient; (2) NEG negative – non-admin receives 403; (3) STC boundary – history endpoint returns the deleted version after soft-delete; (4) BVA boundary – deleting an already-deleted patient returns 410 Gone, confirming idempotent state handling at the 'deleted' boundary.

tests.name: test_admin_soft_delete_patient_returns_200
preconditions: Patient resource exists with known ID and active status
actions: Create a patient via POST /Patient to obtain a fresh ID
expected: Response body (if present) contains meta.tag with system='http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ActReason' and code='PATDEAD' or equivalent soft-delete marker
Resource is NOT physically removed from store

tests.name: test_non_admin_delete_patient_returns_403
preconditions: Patient resource exists with known ID
actions: Create a patient via POST /Patient with admin credentials
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue.severity='error'
trace: R-HLT-004: error pathway – 403 when not admin

tests.name: test_deleted_patient_retrievable_via_history
actions: Send GET /Patient/{id}/_history to retrieve version list
expected: History bundle contains at least one entry
The most recent entry has meta.versionId present and request.method='DELETE' or resource is marked with meta indicating deletion
trace: R-HLT-004: deletion shall not remove history
STC: active -> deleted state transition verified via history endpoint
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "<BASE_URL>"

@pytest.fixture
def admin_headers():
    return <get_auth_headers>(role="admin")

def test_deleted_patient_retrievable_via_history(admin_headers):
    # Arrange: create patient (active state)
    payload = {"resourceType": "Patient", "name": [{"family": "HistoryCheck"}]}
    create_resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient", json=payload, headers=admin_headers)
    assert create_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    patient_id = create_resp.json()["id"]

    # Act: soft-delete (state transition)
    delete_resp = requests.delete(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert delete_resp.status_code in (200, 204)

    # Assert: history preserved
    history_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history",
        headers=admin_headers
    )
    assert history_resp.status_code == 200, (
        f"Expected 200 from _history, got {history_resp.status_code}"
    )
    bundle = history_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("resourceType") == "Bundle"
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "History must contain at least one entry after soft-delete"
    methods = [e.get("request", {}).get("method", "") for e in entries]
    assert "DELETE" in methods or any(
        e.get("resource", {}).get("meta") for e in entries
    ), "Delete event must appear in history"

tests.name: test_delete_already_deleted_patient_returns_410
preconditions: Patient resource exists and has already been soft-deleted
actions: Send DELETE /Patient/{id} again (second delete – boundary: already deleted)
expected: Second DELETE returns HTTP 410 Gone or 200 (idempotent per FHIR R4)
History still intact with both delete attempts recorded or single deletion preserved
trace: BVA: boundary at the 'already deleted' state – testing the edge of the deleted/active partition
R-HLT-004: deletion shall not remove history
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "<BASE_URL>"

@pytest.fixture
def admin_headers():
    return <get_auth_headers>(role="admin")

def test_delete_already_deleted_patient_returns_410(admin_headers):
    # Arrange: create and then soft-delete a patient
    payload = {"resourceType": "Patient", "name": [{"family": "DoubleDelete"}]}
    create_resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient", json=payload, headers=admin_headers)
    assert create_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    patient_id = create_resp.json()["id"]

    first_delete = requests.delete(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert first_delete.status_code in (200, 204), (
        f"First delete failed: {first_delete.status_code}"
    )

    # Act: delete again (boundary – already-deleted state)
    second_delete = requests.delete(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=admin_headers)

    # Assert: must not be a server error; 410 or 200 are both acceptable per FHIR R4 §3.1.0.7
    assert second_delete.status_code in (200, 204, 410), (
        f"Expected 200/204/410 on re-delete, got {second_delete.status_code}"
    )
    assert second_delete.status_code != 500, "Server must not error on re-delete"

    # History must still be accessible
    history_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history",
        headers=admin_headers
    )
    assert history_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(history_resp.json().get("entry", [])) >= 1
